title: wolfhsm crate: idiomatic Rust / RustCrypto API review
description:
## Why
The wolfhsm crate is solid at the FFI level (correct Pin<Box<>>, RAII, Result<> everywhere, safe unsafe) but has several gaps and rough edges that would surprise a RustCrypto user or fail a standard Rust library review. This epic tracks the fixes identified in the initial code review.

## Findings summary
See child issues for details. In priority order:
- P1: Error type naming (WolfHsmError → Error) and semver safety (#[non_exhaustive])
- P2: Missing signature::Signer trait impls for ECC/Ed25519 (biggest composability gap)
- P3: Missing digest::Update/FixedOutput for SHA, aead::AeadInPlace for AES, label ergonomics
- P4: Crate-level docs explaining the with_xxx_key closure pattern

## Acceptance criteria
- All child issues closed
- cargo doc renders meaningful crate-level documentation
- wolfhsm::Error is the public error type
- ECC and Ed25519 key types satisfy signature::Signer bounds
